summary: McKenzie highlights the success of Printer Friendly software after being featured on Bits du Jour, a deal-of-the-day site for software. He references his own more modest experience being featured on the same platform and acknowledges that products with broader mass-market appeal are better positioned to capitalize on such promotional opportunities. The post is a brief, generous congratulation to fellow micro-ISV entrepreneurs.
key_points: Deal-of-the-day sites like Bits du Jour can drive significant sales for independent software vendors
Mass-market appeal is a key factor in whether promotional features translate to blockbuster sales days
The Bits du Jour business model creates win-win relationships between the platform and software vendors
money_quotes: I could definately see the potential for the business model to work with an application with more mass-market appeal.
key_insight: Promotional platforms like deal-of-the-day sites can be powerful sales channels, but their impact scales with how broadly appealing the featured product is.
